title: elaeocarpus euneurus
intro: Elaeocarpus euneurus, also known as the "Blue Marble Tree," is a species of flowering plant native to Queensland, Australia, and New Guinea, belonging to the family Elaeocarpaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and bark, which contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. These compounds are believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, offering various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. In traditional herbal preparations, Elaeocarpus euneurus is often used as a decoction or infusion, made by steeping the leaves or bark in hot water, to harness its medicinal benefits.
